items.summary: The landmark India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will come into force from July 15, 2026, with the government working to ensure that customs notifications and related processes are put in place so that exporters can start availing concessions from day one, senior government sources told ANI.

items.title: Mahindra Logistics plans 350-EV Alyte fleet for Noida International Airport
items.summary: Mahindra Logistics is aiming to grow its fleet from about 70 electric vehicles to nearly 350 during FY27 amid intensifying competition in the premium EV mobility market

items.title: L’Oréal acquires majority stake in Innovist as global giants double down on India
items.summary: Under the agreement, Innovist’s founders Rohit Chawla, Sifat Khurana and Vimal Bhola will retain minority stakes and continue to run the business alongside L’Oréal India

items.title: SBI Board approves ₹60,000 crore fundraising plan for FY27
items.summary: SBI has been actively tapping the bond market in recent months as part of its capital planning

items.title: Nykaa share price hits 52-week high, rises 8% during intraday trading
items.summary: FSN E-Commerce Ventures saw an 8% surge in shares after Nykaa outlined plans for aggressive growth by FY30, with expectations of tripling revenue and robust profitability. The strategy includes expanding its market presence, both online and offline, while increasing sales from owned brands.

items.title: NSE flags regulatory, tech, AI, derivatives concentration risks in IPO papers
items.summary: NSE warns that any further tightening of regulations, higher transaction taxes, shifts in investor preferences or migration towards alternative asset classes could adversely affect trading volumes and profitability

items.title: Bank of Baroda eyes $500 million dollar bond sale under RBI’s hedge window
items.summary: The planned fundraising follows a successful $750 million dollar-bond sale by HDFC Bank earlier this week at a spread of 90 basis points over benchmark US Treasury yields.

items.title: India to allow import of 3.78 lakh UK cars at concessional duty in first 15 years of trade pact
items.summary: India will allow imports of up to 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional duties under a new trade pact starting July 15.  Import duties will fall from 110% to 10% with quotas, while Indian electric and hybrid cars will gain duty-free access to the UK market from the sixth year.

items.title: India to allow import of 3.78 lakh UK cars at concessional duty in first 15 years of trade pact
items.summary: India will allow imports of up to 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional duties under a new trade pact starting July 15.  Import duties will fall from 110% to 10% with quotas, while Indian electric and hybrid cars will gain duty-free access to the UK market from the sixth year.

items.title: Tulip group to invest Rs 6,000 crore in future expansion
items.summary: Tulip Group is set to invest Rs 6,000 crore in Gurugram's residential projects, including the 495-foot-tall Tulip Melrose on Southern Peripheral Road (SPR). This significant investment underscores SPR's emergence as a prime luxury destination, driven by infrastructure upgrades and growing commercial hubs.

items.title: Weak monsoon set to dent India’s 2026-27 coffee prospects
items.summary: Rainfall deficit across key coffee-growing regions raises concerns over crop health and yields

items.title: Indian Oil Corp issues tender to charter LPG carrier from Gulf, document shows
Akasa Air triples borrowing limit to  ₹3,950 crore, eyes government-backed credit scheme
items.summary: The additional borrowing headroom of  ₹2,750 crore will help meet working capital requirements as the airline accelerates expansion.

items.title: NSE files for ₹30,000-crore IPO; set to become India's biggest-ever public issue
items.summary: The filing marks a major milestone for NSE, whose listing plans had been stalled for nearly a decade due to regulatory hurdles, including the co-location controversy

items.title: Bata India appoints Sanjay Rao as MD & CEO
items.summary: Bata India has announced Sanjay Rao will take over as Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer effective on August 24, 2026. He replaces Gunjan Shah. Rao brings over two decades of retail and consumer leadership experience from international markets. Bata Group sees India as a key growth market. Rao's appointment aims to drive consumer relevance and product strength for Bata India.

items.title: Nifty 50 surges 4.5% in 4 sessions as crude oil prices tumble after US-Iran truce: Can the benchmark hit 25,000 in June?
items.summary: The Indian equity market has seen a rebound, driven by easing geopolitical tensions and declining crude prices. Analysts suggest Nifty could reach 25,000 in June if certain conditions are met, but consolidation may occur amid earnings expectations and external factors.

items.title: IFCI, HFCL among 14 stocks that rallied up to 50% in just one month - Do you own any?
items.summary: While benchmark indices moved steadily, broader market momentum surged, with many BSE 500 stocks delivering double-digit gains, several rallying 25–50% in a month, highlighting strong stock-specific performance beneath calm markets.

items.title: Basmati rice exports to West Asia poised for rebound after US, Iran sign peace deal
items.summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.

items.title: BMW to launch 27 products in India this year, eyes No. 1 spot
items.summary: BMW Group India is launching 27 new models this year, aiming to solidify its position as the top luxury carmaker. The company is focusing on expanding its portfolio and meeting customer demand, expecting strong double-digit growth.  MINI sales are also projected to significantly increase, with plans for network expansion.

items.title: Rupee’s oil relief capped by RBI's FX book, interest payment hedges, bankers say
items.summary:
RBI's short-dollar forward book is
estimated to have ​ballooned to an all-time high of nearly $110
billion

items.title: NSE flags regulatory, tech, AI, derivatives concentration risks in IPO papers
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange raises a red flag regarding substantial financial and operational threats. Emerging regulatory frameworks, tech breakdowns, cyber threats, and the rise of AI present notable concerns. The NSE's dependency on derivatives revenue leaves it exposed. Recent adjustments from Sebi have already dampened trading volumes, and stricter regulations could further constrict both activity and profit margins.

items.title: Global energy transition readiness dips, India among top gainers: WEF
items.summary: Global energy transition readiness has declined for the first time in over a decade. Geopolitical risks are a major factor. However, India has registered one of the strongest improvements. India advanced two places to rank 70th on the World Economic Forum's Energy Transition Index 2026. This progress is attributed to infrastructure gains and improvements in equity, sustainability, and financial investment.

items.title: El Nino fear pushes farmers in Srikakulam and Vizianagaram towards water-saving paddy methods
items.summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are shifting to water-saving rice farming techniques. Facing potential El Nino impacts, cultivators are embracing Direct Seeded Rice and Alternate Wetting and Drying. These methods significantly reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is actively promoting these changes, alongside soil health improvements, to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

items.title: Extended summer may lift AC sales, but growth likely to fall short of expectations: Praveen Sahay
items.summary: India's room air conditioner market anticipates a boost from an extended summer and El Niño, though growth is tempered by conservative dealer inventory stocking. While retail demand has been strong, lower primary sales to dealers have capped overall industry expansion, impacting expected growth figures.

items.title: SBI to raise Rs 60,000 cr in FY27 via bonds
items.summary: State Bank of India's board has approved raising up to Rs 60,000 crore in the current fiscal year through debt instruments. These funds will be raised via long-term bonds, Basel III-compliant Additional Tier 1, and Tier 2 Bonds. The issuance will be through public offer or private placement to Indian and overseas investors during FY27.

items.title: Pranjul Bhandari: Temperature trends tell us more about future food inflation than rainfall levels do
items.summary: Temperature has become a better predictor than rainfall of India’s farm sector-driven economic outcomes. And our heat problem worsens in El Niño years. As we enter a season of overlapping shocks, estimates for 2026-27 show the damage we can expect.

items.title: Indian state fuel retailers hitting borrowing limits, says official
items.summary: Indian state fuel companies are facing financial strain. They are borrowing more money as they sell petrol, diesel, and LPG at prices lower than the market. Revenue losses for these companies reached one trillion rupees in the first quarter of this year. While other nations increased fuel prices significantly, India's price hikes have been modest.

items.title: How behemoth NSE's listing could benefit rival BSE
items.summary: The marquee bourse’s debut on BSE could provide a long-awaited boost to the latter’s cash-market volumes, stuck at 6-8% for years.

items.title: A 70-year-old law and a 10% stake: What keeps LIC invested in NSE
items.summary: NSE IPO: Maintaining a significant stake in the exchange works in LIC's favour, granting it the power to hold a director's seat on the company's board

items.title: Why The LaLiT parent Bharat Hotels will focus on consolidation before reviving IPO plans
items.summary: Bharat Hotels plans to grow through management contracts and joint developments rather than asset acquisitions, while keeping its IPO ambitions alive for the next 2-3 years.

items.title: $15 billion game-changer: Reliance Jio readies for a big bang in space
items.summary: Reliance Jio is reportedly planning a massive 1,600-satellite low Earth orbit constellation, aiming for direct-to-device connectivity and extending its telecom dominance into space. This ambitious project, potentially costing $10-15 billion, seeks to bolster India's digital sovereignty and reduce reliance on foreign infrastructure, positioning Jio as a major global player.

items.title: ADIA, Aberdeen Group, others buy 7.3% stake in Corona Remedies for ₹777 crore
items.summary: Following the stake purchase, shares of Corona Remedies rose nearly 3 per cent on Thursday to trade at ₹1,840 apiece on the National Stock Exchange (NSE).

items.title: ​Rusk Media raises Rs 100 crore Pre-Series C round led by Nazara Technologies​
items.summary: Rusk Media has secured Rs 100 crore in a Pre-Series C funding round led by Nazara Technologies, with participation from Info Edge Ventures, IvyCap Ventures, and Audacity VC. The capital will fuel content expansion, scale its Alright! TV platform, and advance AI-powered production technologies.

items.title: Govt may expand construction equipment localization scheme to include port infra, container components
items.summary: Originally proposed in the FY27 budget with  ₹200 crore allocated, discussions focus on incentivizing local manufacturing due to rising demand in construction and shipbuilding sectors.

items.title: Bitcoin trades near $64,000 despite hawkish Fed outlook; recovery attempt loses momentum
items.summary: Bitcoin hovered near $64,000 as a hawkish Federal Reserve outlook and risk-off sentiment capped recovery momentum. Liquidations, ETF outflows and weak institutional participation weighed on prices. Key support lies between $61,000 and $63,500, while resistance near $67,500 remains a hurdle for any sustained upside in the near term.

items.title: India weighs extending 40-product import duty exemption beyond June 30 amid West Asia tensions
items.summary: “A decision would be taken after analysing the evolving situation around West Asia and cargo movement via Strait of Hormuz,” sources said

items.title: Smallcap stock jumps 24% in a week as NSE stake could be valued at Rs 850 crore
items.summary: Maithan Alloys shares saw a significant jump on Thursday. This rally follows the filing of the National Stock Exchange's draft prospectus for its initial public offering. Maithan Alloys holds a stake in NSE, which is now valued at approximately Rs 850 crore. The NSE IPO is expected to be India's largest ever.

items.title: Rupee hits six-week high on offshore, exporter flows
items.summary: The Indian Rupee saw a remarkable resurgence on Thursday, hitting a six-week high after a period of weakness influenced by the Federal Reserve's strict monetary stance. This turnaround was fueled by increased dollar selling from foreign markets and robust exporter contributions. Furthermore, the recent downturn in oil prices offered additional support.

items.title: Reliance AGM 2026: Jio IPO, AI strategy, new energy roadmap among key triggers in focus
items.summary: Reliance AGM 2026: The biggest area of investor interest is expected to be any announcement regarding the public listing of Jio Platforms. Beyond the Jio IPO, analysts expect Reliance's artificial intelligence (AI) strategy to emerge as a major theme during the AGM.

items.title: Gold price crashes Rs 2,300 on MCX, Silver slides nearly Rs 7,000 — Why are precious metals falling?
items.summary: Gold and silver prices fell sharply on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) on Thursday, with gold futures dropping over Rs 2,300 and silver futures sliding nearly Rs 7,000. Analysts attributed the decline to uncertainty over the US Federal Reserve's policy outlook, easing geopolitical tensions, and profit-booking after the recent rally in precious metals.

items.title: Tulip Infratech to invest Rs 1,100 cr on construction of luxury housing project in Gurugram
items.summary: Tulip Infratech is making a bold move with a staggering investment of Rs 1,100 crore for a new upscale residential venture in Gurugram, aptly named Tulip Melrose. This luxurious development will boast over 600 exquisite units located along the vibrant Southern Peripheral Road, with construction slated to unfold over the next four years.

items.title: Gold imports fall to 25-30 tonnes/month from 70-100 tonnes after duty hike: Govt source
items.summary: Gold imports into India have dropped sharply.  The government's decision to increase import duty is proving effective.  Higher gold prices and reduced jewellery demand also contributed to this trend.  Increased global gold recycling is another factor.  Official gold inflows have also moderated.  The government is not pursuing gold monetisation due to past challenges.

items.title: India-UK Trade Deal: Why Indian textile exporters could benefit big—See winners list
items.summary: India and the UK recently announced that a trade deal between them will take effect on July 15. India will receive duty-free access on about 99 per cent of tariff lines, while India will reduce tariffs on around 90 per cent of tariff lines in a phased manner. A Double Contribution Convention (DCC) is also set to come into force on the same day, reinforcing the mobility and competitiveness of Indian professionals in the UK. Here's how analysts view these developments.

items.title: Coforge’s FY30 plan is bold. Now it needs global macros to turn favourable for IT sector
items.summary: Coforge aims to double revenue to $5 billion by FY30 through 15% organic growth, AI-led opportunities and acquisitions such as Encora. Strong deal wins and a growing order book support the outlook, though macroeconomic uncertainty, AI disruption and integration risks could challenge execution.

items.title: L’Oreal set to acquire majority stake in Innovist
items.summary: Transaction expected to close within a few months. L’Oréal has also secured rights to buy out the minority shareholders in full.

items.title: Rupee’s oil relief capped by RBI's FX book, interest payment hedges, bankers say
items.summary: The Indian rupee's upward surge encounters some obstacles. As the Reserve Bank of India gradually dissolves a hefty foreign exchange forward book, banks are also preparing for interest payments on foreign currency holdings. These maneuvers are likely to soak up dollar inflows, which could limit the rupee's rise. Analysts predict a cautious outlook for the currency's future gains.

items.title: Nykaa shares jump 8% to fresh 52-week high after unveiling FY30 roadmap; targets up to 3x revenue growth
items.summary: Nykaa's parent company, FSN E-Commerce Ventures, saw its shares surge over 8% to a 52-week high after unveiling an ambitious FY30 vision. The company aims to achieve a $5 billion gross merchandise value, driven by disciplined execution and capital-efficient investments.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei gauge crosses 71,000 on US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei share index soared to a historic 71,000 level on Thursday. This surge followed a ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran. The U.S. Federal Reserve's stance also influenced markets. Chip-related and financial stocks led the gains. Marine transportation stocks saw a decline. The Nikkei 225 index reached an intraday high of 71,293.64.

items.title: Strengthening grid integration of captive TPPs can substitute for capacity additions: Study
items.summary: Latest CEEW study says improving utilisation of industrial power units can cut stranded asset risks and support renewable integration

items.title: HDFC Bank shares gain over 2% as chairman appointment clarity boosts sentiment
items.summary: Investors cheer signs of progress in HDFC Bank's chairman appointment process as the stock emerges among the top contributors to the Nifty 50's gains.

items.title: Edtech funding revival offers hope, but sector still faces a long road ahead
items.summary: Investments into the country's edtech firms surged to $178 million in the first half of calendar 2026 from $73.3 million in the same period last year. This is also higher than $155 million raised in entire 2025, according to data intelligence platform Tracxn.

items.title: Stocks to buy: Nagaraj Shetti recommends IRFC, Lloyds Enterprises shares to buy in the short-term
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices saw marginal gains as easing oil prices and improved geopolitical conditions countered US Federal Reserve concerns. Brent crude dropped 1.7% to $78.2 per barrel. Most sectoral indices were positive, but IT stocks fell 1.5% amid rate hike signals from the Fed.

items.title: Gujarat Gas gets NSE, BSE nod for listing of 62.27 crore shares; stock in focus
items.summary: The share allotment was carried out pursuant to the composite scheme of amalgamation and arrangement involving GSPC, GSPL, GSPC Energy Ltd, Gujarat Energy Ltd and GSPL Transmission Ltd.

items.title: Gold imports drop 70% to 30 tonnes after duty hike: Official
items.summary: However, in value terms, India’s gold imports rose by 34% year-on-year to $3.41 billion in May, driven by higher prices of the precious metal

items.title: Infosys, TCS, Wipro, other IT stocks fall up to 3% after Fed's hawkish tone. What's ahead?
items.summary: Indian IT stocks, including Infosys and TCS, experienced a significant decline of up to 3% on Thursday. This downturn was triggered by a hawkish stance from the US Federal Reserve, which has increased expectations of a rate hike later this year. Concerns over reduced discretionary spending in North America, a key market for these companies, are driving the sell-off.

items.title: Stay invested, keep accumulating quality stocks: Neeraj Dewan
items.summary: Easing crude oil prices have boosted sentiment for Indian equities, with experts recommending accumulation of quality stocks in financials, defence, infrastructure, and metals. Investors are advised to use market dips as buying opportunities, especially in mid and small-cap segments. While inflation and monsoon concerns persist, they are seen as chances to invest.

items.title: Telcos, consumer groups spar over Trai's telecom grievance overhaul
items.summary: Telcos have opposed penalties, 24x7 complaint centres and language mandates, while consumer advocates seek stronger safeguards and easier access to human support.

items.title: Govt. ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
items.summary: Earlier on March 2, the CBT in a meeting chaired by the Union Labour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya had decided to fix 8.25% rate of interest for the financial year 2025-26, marking the third consecutive year this rate has been maintained

items.title: UKIBC hails India-UK trade pact rollout from July 15, says CETA to unlock new growth opportunities
items.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement, CETA, will become effective on July 15, 2026. This landmark deal opens new avenues for trade and investment between India and the United Kingdom. Businesses are encouraged to prepare for its implementation. The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.

items.title: Multibagger defence stock Paras Defence jumps 5% to hit record high. Do you own?
items.summary: Paras Defence share price hit record high in intra-day deals today. The stock has seen remarkable growth, reflecting robust operational momentum and an expanding domestic defence manufacturing landscape.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty advance cautiously amid IT sell-off, gains in defence, banking stocks lend support
items.summary: Investor sentiment remained supported by lower oil prices after progress in the US-Iran peace process, which has lifted the markets in the last four sessions

items.title: Understanding risk is enough for learning all about returns: Smart investor's edge
items.summary: Ever wondered what really happens when you attempt to reverse-engineer returns without dedicating sufficient time to understanding risks? Most financial experts will give a number of reasons why one should avoid the chase for sky-high returns and, instead, focus on the basics over the long term.

items.title: Govt ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
items.summary: The government has approved an 8.25% interest rate on Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) deposits for FY26, clearing the proposal recommended by the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's (EPFO) Central Board of Trustees in March. The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

items.title: SpaceX's historic IPO rally hits first speed bump – What's next for investors?
items.summary: SpaceX's record-breaking IPO sparked a massive rally before shares experienced their first pullback. Despite profit booking and valuation concerns, investors remain focused on Starlink, AI, and long-term growth opportunities.

items.title: Raamdeo Agrawal, Radhakishan Damani, Motilal Oswal, Dolly Khanna: The superstar investors sitting out the NSE IPO
items.summary: While public sector entities like SBI cash in on decades-old NSE holdings for massive profits, prominent individual investors including Radhakishan Damani, Sunil Kant Munjal, and S. Gopalakrishnan are opting to retain their substantial stakes. LIC, NSE's largest shareholder, also remains a holdout, contrasting with other institutions realizing significant gains from the exchange's blockbuster IPO.

items.title: L’Oréal buys control of Innovist as India beauty battle intensifies
items.summary: L'Oréal SA is acquiring a majority stake in Indian beauty startup Innovist. This move strengthens L'Oréal's position in India's rapidly expanding personal care market. Innovist, known for its science-led brands like Bare Anatomy, will continue to be run by its founders. This partnership aims to capture younger, digitally-savvy Indian consumers.

items.title: The difference between investing and trading
items.summary: Many, many market participants believe they are investing when, in reality, they are only trading -- and are far from truly investing -- far more frequently than they realise! This distinction lies not just in how often you buy or sell, but also in the time horizon, strategy and risk involved. NSE’s ‘Soch Kar, Samajh Kar, Invest Kar’ slogan enables investors to make smarter, wiser and well-informed decisions in their wealth creation journey.

items.title: Robinhood stock soars despite layoffs – Why Wall Street is bullish
items.summary: Robinhood shares rallied after announcing a 10% workforce reduction, as investors viewed the move as a cost-efficiency strategy. Strong trading activity and analyst upgrades further boosted confidence in long-term profitability.

items.title: NSE's long road to Dalal Street: How scandals and lawsuits delayed India's biggest IPO
items.summary: After years of regulatory probes, court battles and governance controversies stalled its listing ambitions, NSE has finally filed for what could become India's largest-ever public offering.

items.title: YouTube is now India's prime-time screen, says Gunjan Soni
items.summary: YouTube has become India's primary viewing platform, blending online reality with high-quality video. The company is significantly growing its presence on Connected TVs, reaching millions of adults. YouTube is also seeing substantial success in its commerce initiatives, with many users searching for shopping content. India's creator economy is a key driver of national GDP and global pop culture.

items.title: Prime Video eyes greater audience scale through unified AVOD-SVOD service in India
items.summary: Prime Video is merging its free and paid streaming services in India. This integration aims to significantly boost audience numbers by combining Prime Video's premium viewers with Amazon MX Player's wide reach. The move creates India's largest streaming service for exclusive originals. This unified platform offers creators and advertisers unparalleled scale across free and paid content.

items.title: How could NSE IPO's  ₹5 lakh crore valuation impact Dalal Street's market cap?
items.summary: NSE's draft papers filing marks a significant milestone for Indian capital markets, which could not only attract foreign capital but also boost the overall market capitalisation by nearly 1%.

items.title: Are Indians who are hard-up for money looking at illegal online games for financial relief?
items.summary: A sharp rise in the cost of living may be driving people to view online gaming as an opportunity to fill income gaps by using what they might see as their skills. Given India’s ban on money games, this is risky behaviour. Can a solution be crafted?

items.title: D2C brands turn to WhatsApp to win new customers
items.summary: WhatsApp is increasingly becoming a customer acquisition channel for India’s D2C brands rather than just a platform for customer support and retention. According to a GoKwik report, 83% of orders generated through WhatsApp during the October-December festive quarter came from first-time buyers.

items.title: Yes Bank shares jump 16% in 5 days, hit fresh 52-week high. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Yes Bank shares climbed to a fresh 52-week high, extending gains over five sessions and adding Rs 8,662 crore to market value. The rally followed its partnership with Northern Arc Capital. Analysts remain cautious despite improved technical momentum and stronger Q4 earnings and asset quality.

items.title: Gujarat Energy expects 30% growth in gas trading by 2030-31 despite FY26 volume drop
items.summary: GEL expects steady annual profitability of Rs 1,000–1,100 crore while strengthening its LNG sourcing base through long-term contracts with global suppliers such as QatarEnergy and Uniper.

items.title: Last chance to buy! Bluechips HDFC Bank, Tata Motors PV among stocks turning ex-dividend tomorrow
items.summary: HDFC Bank and Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles will trade ex-dividend on June 19. Investors must buy shares by June 18 to qualify for dividends, totaling  ₹248 per share across companies. HDFC Bank offers  ₹13 per share, while Tata Motors offers  ₹3, among others.

items.title: JAL shares delist from BSE and NSE today: Will its 6.5 lakh shareholders get anything?
items.summary: Jaiprakash Associates shares were delisted from the BSE and NSE on Thursday, marking the closure of one of India's longest-running insolvency cases. The delisting follows the acquisition of the debt-laden company's assets by Adani Group. JAL said it had received final approval from both exchanges and thanked them for their support during its listing tenure.

items.title: Court sends former Reliance MD Gautam Doshi in judicial custody till July 1 in ₹40,000 cr RCom money laundering case
items.summary: It is alleged against Gautam Doshi that he occupied a position of substantial authority and control within the Reliance ADA Group and was deeply associated with its financial, corporate and offshore operations

items.title: JBM Auto secures Rs 750 crore investment for e-bus deployment
items.summary: JBM Auto's unit has secured a significant Rs 750 crore long-term investment from Motilal Oswal to deploy 2,000 e-buses and bolster India's sustainable public transit. This substantial capital infusion will accelerate the rollout of zero-emission fleets across key Indian cities, marking a major step in the nation's electric mobility transition.

items.title: Iran-U.S. MoU opens doors for Iran to stake claims in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: While Iran had presented a toll on the Strait of Hormuz as a means of funding post-war reconstruction, the agreement provides for a separate massive reconstruction fund in addition to oil-sale revenues

items.title: Vedanta Iron and Steel shares hit 5% upper circuit for fourth straight day, surge 20% since listing
items.summary: Vedanta Iron & Steel shares hit the 5% upper circuit for a fourth straight session, extending gains to over 20% since listing. The rally has been fuelled by strong investor interest, Premji Invest’s Rs 102-crore bulk deal, and optimism that Vedanta’s demerger could unlock value in the standalone business.

items.title: IndiGo launches ‘Cabs with IndiGo’ airport transfer service across Indian cities
items.summary: IndiGo has introduced 'Cabs with IndiGo', an airport transfer service. Customers can now book cabs to and from airports across India. The service is available on the IndiGo website and mobile app. This expansion aims to enhance IndiGo's travel ecosystem. BluChip loyalty members will earn rewards on bookings. The service offers fixed pricing and flexible cancellation options.

items.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO: Price band set at  ₹769-808 per share; check key dates, issue details
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism Limited sets its IPO price band between  ₹769 to  ₹808 per share, with subscription from June 23 to June 25. Reservations include 75% for QIBs, 15% for NIIs, and 10% for retail investors. Shares will list on BSE and NSE on July 1.

items.title: India-UK FTA levels the playing field for apparel exporters, says Pearl Global's Pallab Banerjee
items.summary: India's apparel exports are set for a significant boost with the new UK Free Trade Agreement, finally removing a long-standing 10-12% tariff disadvantage against competitors like Bangladesh and Cambodia. Industry leaders anticipate improved price competitiveness, attracting fresh investments, increasing exports, and creating substantial employment opportunities across various garment categories.

items.title: India must boost local production of electrical equipment or resign to import dependency
items.summary: Business-as-usual would leave India with an electrical equipment import dependence that may rival the energy sector’s within a decade. Here's what could turn the country into an electrical goods powerhouse.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech’s Rs 883 crore IPO opens tomorrow: GMP, price band & other key details
items.summary: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions launches its Rs 883 crore IPO on June 19. The price band is Rs 144-152 per share. The IPO includes a fresh issue and an offer-for-sale. Proceeds will strengthen infrastructure and fund growth. Turtlemint simplifies insurance policy purchase and management for customers and advisors.

items.title: Sports remains biggest audience aggregator in an era of infinite content: JioStar's Ishan Chatterjee
items.summary: Live sports remains a powerful audience magnet, reaching over a billion viewers during the IPL. JioStar is using this scale to expand across sports, commerce, and AI-driven fan experiences.  The company sees significant growth in regional languages and non-cricket sports like Kabaddi.  Future plans include AI-powered, voice-led fan interactions.

items.title: Rupee falls 21 paise to 94.71 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: The rupee depreciated 21 paise to 94.71 against the US dollar due to the US Federal Reserve's hawkish stance and a strong dollar index. Asian currencies also weakened, impacting the rupee. Meanwhile, positive sentiment emerged from reports of a US-Iran memorandum of understanding and ongoing trade talks between India and the US.

items.title: US Fed's hawkish tone shuts door on India bond rally for now
items.summary: Indian government bonds saw a slight dip as the U.S. Federal Reserve adopted a more hawkish stance, signaling potential rate hikes this year due to persistent inflation risks. This shift influenced Treasury yields, while foreign investors continued to pour funds into Indian bonds, attracted by central bank measures.

items.title: Hyundai Motor to LIC: Ahead of NSE, Reliance Jio IPOs, here's a look at 4 largest-ever Indian public offerings
items.summary: NSE's IPO is likely to be worth $3.3 billion, based on its share price in private markets, and comes after years of regulatory delays. Existing investors will sell 6% of the company's equity as part of the issue, which will be a pure offer-for-sale with no fresh equity being raised.

items.title: BSE among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks saw futures open interest jump over 11% on June 17, led by Nuvama Wealth Management, indicating heightened trader participation, fresh position creation, and growing market interest.

items.title: US Stock Market: Warsh signals overhaul of policy communications, skips rate projection in major shift
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh signalled a possible overhaul of the US central bank’s communication strategy after declining to submit his own interest-rate forecast in the latest SEP. items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Gold fell to the lowest level in a month primarily as the dollar rose against its major crosses and better-than-expected economic number from the US.

items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Base metals traded choppy last week, with prices hitting multi month highs at the start of the week and followed by some profit taking on back of volatility in the dollar and mixed data signals from China and US.

items.title: Pixel launch: Is Google Assistant's AI an assault on privacy?
items.summary: Sure, artificial intelligence can make life simpler by suggesting you places to eat, tell you weather, or even crack a joke.  The more you use it, the more it learns about your habits and preferences. The protocols pertaining to artificial intelligence, or the lack of it, make our data less secure and more vulnerable.
